Content_Sketch: The call comes in. You haven't arrived yet. You already know who it is.
Illustration_Note: Cop in patrol car, MDT screen glowing, hand near holster, camera blinking red

Content_Sketch: Not because you know them.
Illustration_Note: POV from patrol car looking through windshield at dark neighborhood street

Content_Sketch: You don't protect people. You sort them.
Illustration_Note: Stickman cop silhouette against city lights, badge visible, weight line overlay

Content_Sketch: Training room. Instructor before class. 'I feared for my life' - taught like a formula. Before teaching aim.
Illustration_Note: Training room, instructor at whiteboard with five words written, rows of recruits sitting

Content_Sketch: First patrol. Weapon heavy on hip. Hope you never use it.
Illustration_Note: Cop walking first beat, new uniform, shiny badge, hand away from holster, weapon looks heavy

Content_Sketch: Hand touches holster. Notice. Pull back.
Illustration_Note: Close on hand touching holster then pulling away, cop looking down at own hand

Content_Sketch: First draw. Unarmed person. They freeze. Everything stops. Except you. You decide what happens next.
Illustration_Note: Cop with weapon drawn, civilian frozen hands up, everything still, cop standing tall

Content_Sketch: You wait for the fear. It doesn't come.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's face, dot eyes neutral, hand steady on weapon, no shaking

Content_Sketch: 'Show your hands. Get on the ground.' Slow. Deliberate. Pause between commands.
Illustration_Note: Cop giving commands to civilian on sidewalk, civilian complying slowly, measured scene

Content_Sketch: 'You reach before you think.' First time. Notice. Pull hand back.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's hand already on holster, cop looking at own hand, pulling it back

Content_Sketch: Draw again. Faster. They haven't stopped yet. Already shouting. Everything stops. Except you.
Illustration_Note: Cop drawing weapon fast, civilian mid-step, commands overlapping, intensity rising

Content_Sketch: Don't wait for them to stop. Don't wait for them to understand.
Illustration_Note: POV of cop approaching figure on sidewalk at night, hand on holster, streetlight glare

Content_Sketch: 'Hands up. On the ground. Don't move.' Overlapping. Fire. Board clears. Forty minutes for a life.
Illustration_Note: Split: cop firing on sidewalk | review board room, three faceless figures behind desk, folders

Content_Sketch: 'Furtive movement.' Already wrote that before. Don't remember when.
Illustration_Note: Cop at precinct desk writing report, same words on page, stack of old reports beside

Content_Sketch: Shooting count highest in precinct. Sergeant calls you 'proactive.' Except you.
Illustration_Note: Precinct whiteboard with stats, cop's name at top, sergeant nodding, cop standing apart

Content_Sketch: 'I feared for my life.' Automatic. No thought. Camera click. Don't look.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's mouth forming words mechanically, body camera clicking off without looking

Content_Sketch: 'Show your ID.' They reach for wallet. 'Hands-' Fire.
Illustration_Note: Sequence: cop commanding, civilian reaching for pocket, cop's hand snapping up, muzzle flash

Content_Sketch: 'Show your ID.' It was already in their hand. Don't remember deciding to fire.
Illustration_Note: Close on fallen civilian, ID card visible in open hand, photo facing up, cop standing over

Content_Sketch: Rookie hesitates. You don't. Rookie fires after you.
Illustration_Note: Two cops - rookie frozen then firing after subject, three weapons pointed at one target

Content_Sketch: Rookie looks at you. The way you used to look at someone else.
Illustration_Note: Rookie staring at cop, same wide-eyed look cop had in training, mirror moment

Content_Sketch: Teach rookie the five words. 'I feared for my life.' Rookie's voice. Sounds wrong.
Illustration_Note: Cop standing over rookie at desk, rookie repeating words, cop listening, expression empty

Content_Sketch: Look at report. Recognize the words. Not the person.
Illustration_Note: Cop at desk reading report, familiar handwriting, ID photo in corner of file

Content_Sketch: ID photo in corner of file. Don't remember seeing that face.
Illustration_Note: Close on file with ID photo, cop's hand resting on page, unfocused stare

Content_Sketch: Read report again. One detail wrong. Don't fix it.
Illustration_Note: Close on report text, one line clearly incorrect, pen beside it untouched

Content_Sketch: 'I feared for my life.' Don't remember if you did.
Illustration_Note: Close on cop's face, dot eyes empty, mouth forming words without meaning

Content_Sketch: The call comes in. Same format. Different person. Hand on holster before dispatcher finishes.
Illustration_Note: Same patrol car as HOOK, same MDT, same posture, hand on holster, camera absent

Content_Sketch: Hand was already there. You didn't place it.
Illustration_Note: Close on hand on holster, cop looking forward, not down - hand permanent, identity complete
